raw-backfill: AI-research ingest. The select_raw_batch.py digest above lists unprocessed raw files. Your FIRST response MUST be a tool call (file_read on the first raw file, or $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md for the workflow). For each file, run the §Ingest workflow in $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md: create a source page (source_kind: paper/lab-post/commentary; published) keyed by `raw:`; extract entities worth tracking over time (model / lab / researcher / method / benchmark / dataset) and cross-link them; file a prediction only for an explicit, falsifiable, dated claim. WRITE via the MCP write path (mcp_okengine_write_create_entity / update_entity), NOT file_write. A single incremental paper is usually a source + a method cross-link, NOT its own entity. LOCAL-ONLY: do not call web_search/web_extract/web_crawl. Append one wiki/log.md summary line.
entity-backfill: AI-research entity backfill. The select_entity_candidates.py digest above lists existing entity pages + recent sources. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, create or update entity pages (model / lab / researcher / benchmark / dataset) for subjects worth tracking over time; dedupe against existing; cross-link model↔lab↔researcher↔method↔benchmark. WRITE via the MCP write path; for LIST fields like `sources:` send the COMPLETE updated list (read first, append, send all). Do NOT create concepts or predictions here. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
concept-backfill: AI-research concept backfill. The select_concept_candidates.py digest above lists missing concept slugs (wikilinked but no page). FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, create concept/method pages (a method, architecture, or research theme such as mixture-of-experts or retrieval-augmentation) synthesized from the citing sources. If a slug is really a model/lab/person name, it is an ENTITY not a concept — skip and note it. WRITE via mcp_okengine_write_create_entity. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
source-quality-backfill: Source enrichment. The select_unscored_sources.py digest above lists source pages missing key fields. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, complete each by UPDATING FRONTMATTER ONLY via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity (pass frontmatter_yaml, NO body — the body is preserved): set source_kind (paper/lab-post/commentary), published, and authors/lab if evident from the page. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
schema-classify-drain: Type classification. The select_schema_classify.py digest above lists entities typed generically that need a canonical type. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per the $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md taxonomy, set `type:` to the single best canonical type (model / lab / researcher / method / benchmark / dataset) via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity (frontmatter merge) — or leave the page alone if no canonical type honestly fits. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
page-quality-enrich: Deepen thin pages. The select_page_quality_enrich.py digest above lists deficient entity/concept pages with the source pages that cite each. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, AUGMENT each target — append 2-4 `##` sections of real analysis grounded ONLY in the citing sources — via mcp_okengine_write_append_to_section. Never replace existing content; the field-loss guard protects curated frontmatter. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
trends-refresh: AI-research trend synthesis. The select_trend_deltas.py digest above lists entities (models, labs, methods, benchmarks) whose recent source-mention frequency rose sharply over the rolling window. Synthesize 1-3 grounded `trend` pages at wiki/trends/<slug> (frontmatter: type: trend, title, period: <e.g. '2026-05..2026-06'>, direction: <up|down|shift>, updated: <TODAY YYYY-MM-DD>) — each a DATED, DIRECTIONAL observation over the window (a capability/adoption/research shift), NOT a timeless concept or a daily snapshot. Use [[wikilinks]] to the driving entities + their recent sources and one line on why it's moving. Ground each in the underlying entities first (retrieve_context / search). Write via the MCP write path; skip noise. LOCAL-ONLY. End with a one-line summary.
daily-brief:
Daily AI-research brief. Your FIRST response MUST be a tool call: file_read on wiki/HOT.md (the derived load-first set: recent sources, recently-updated entities, open predictions). Then write today's brief via mcp_okengine_write_create_entity (the enforced write path — never file_write) to path "briefings/daily-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md" (frontmatter: type: briefing, title, published: <YYYY-MM-DD>, updated: <YYYY-MM-DD> — use TODAY's actual date, not a guessed or future one, for the filename and both date fields) covering the most significant AI-research developments since yesterday — new models/capabilities, notable papers/techniques, benchmark movements, credible release signals — each 1-2 sentences with [[wikilinks]] to the entities + their source. Note any open prediction that moved today. If little or nothing significant landed, say so in one line rather than padding. Practitioner-grade and terse; skip 101 explanation. LOCAL-ONLY: do not use web tools.

The select_daily_brief.py digest above lists the window's fresh sources, entity movement, due predictions, and new gaps — TRUST THE DIGEST; do not tour the vault or HOT.md for discovery.
